items.title: More than 100,000 Tasmanians moved to different electorates

children.content: More than a quarter of Tasmanians will vote in new electorates under a long-awaited boundary redistribution announced by the Australian Electoral Commission.

items.title: LGH almost full as patients told not to come unless its an emergency

children.content: Tasmania's top health official says it will take the Launceston General Hospital "two or three days" to bring its services back to an acceptable level, after it issued a notice urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

image.altText: Exteriors of a public hospital.

items.title: Tasmanian government wants to buy Boag's Brewery for 'fair price'

children.content: The Tasmanian government wants to buy Launceston's Boag's Brewery site and says it has "in-principle" support from the Japanese parent company to explore the idea.

image.altText: Beer bottles on a bench

items.title: New owner of Rushy Lagoon reveals plans for Tasmania's largest farm

children.content: The buyer of Tasmania's largest farm says in addition to the pine plantation it has planned for the property, it's also considering cropping and leasing out parts of the farm for grazing, tourism and mountain biking.

items.title: Woman sues ex-Catholic archbishop over alleged abuse by another priest

children.content: A Tasmanian woman is suing former Archbishop of Hobart Julian Porteous and the state of Tasmania over allegations of sexual abuse by two others when she was a child.

image.altText: Julian Porteous stands in front of a full church.

items.title: Influencer stunts are no guide to winter bushwalking, authorities warn hikers

articles.title: One-way 'speedboats with explosives' launched by US for first time

children.content: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

articles.title: Trump ditches plan for 20 per cent toll on Strait of Hormuz shipping
